reason: Global RVV authority chain, Stage2 boundary, and no legacy route authority

Provider-owned route validation contract and metadata mirror patterns

change: Added provider-owned RVVVectorReductionRouteValidationContract and type-mapping contract surface for the existing ReduceAdd + VectorRHSLoad route family.

Built vector-reduction validation contracts from provider-owned typed config, runtime ABI, operand binding, header/type, target leaf, intrinsic, layout, and AVL/VL facts rather than mutable target-local description mirrors.
Populated vector-reduction runtime control, target leaf, provider-supported, header, and C type mapping mirrors during route analysis and metadata generation.

Rewired vector-reduction target artifact validation to consume the provider contract for typed route facts, runtime ABI/order, ABI mappings, route payload, headers/type mappings, statement-plan expectations, and candidate mirrors.
Added provider-interface coverage for vector-reduction validation contract fields and cross-family rejection.
Added target-consumer coverage for vector-reduction contract mirrors and fail-closed stale provider/candidate fields.

Fixed contract construction so stale runtime ABI/order and operand-binding description mutations no longer become the contract authority; split vector-reduction diagnostics for operand binding and reduce_add intrinsic failures.
